context: Need a consistent operational-status flow for new tenant/location creation and period-based auto-reading so 'belum beroperasi' is handled without breaking dashboard/workspace/detail views or Supabase queries.
options_considered: Keep manual status only and store 'belum_beroperasi' as a display flag
Derive status from dates only in the UI
Derive status from dates in shared helpers and API aggregations
decision: Treat contract period dates as authoritative for customer operational status. New locations are created as 'belum_beroperasi' when period start is in the future, automatically flip to 'aktif' when the period begins, and become 'expired' after end date. Propagate the same resolver to app utilities, dashboard/workspace/detail views, and API aggregations/notifications.
reasoning: Dates already define the business lifecycle, so deriving from them prevents stale pending statuses and keeps the create form, detail pages, dashboard, and notifications in sync. Centralizing the resolver avoids the mismatch that caused pending locations to be treated as active in some paths.

context: Audit fix for tenant contract renewal flow and Supabase error handling
options_considered: Keep warning-only error handling and rely on UI refresh
Create renewal version automatically with full dates/invoices in follow-up update
Create draft renewal version only and generate invoices after manual period completion
Use database transaction/RPC for contract version update and invoice generation
decision: Fail fast on Supabase errors during contract version parent sync and invoice generation; make renewal draft creation idempotent by reusing an existing empty higher-number contract version; clean lint-triggering empty catch blocks and trailing whitespace.
reasoning: The current business flow expects manual completion of renewal period after tenant response. Reusing existing draft avoids duplicate blank versions on retries. Throwing Supabase errors prevents false success states when contract/invoice sync fails. A full transaction/RPC would be stronger but requires backend schema/function work beyond this focused frontend/API-layer fix.

context: Monitoring billing month coloring for non-monthly/custom month billing cycles.
options_considered: Change only table rendering in MonitoringSpreadsheetPage.jsx
Change monitoring API row months generation in frontend/src/lib/api.js
Change invoice generation schedule
decision: Implemented coverage in monitoringApi.getBilling: contracts now fetch billing_every/billing_unit, and paid non-monthly invoice cells mark the months after the previous scheduled invoice through the paid invoice month as lunas.
reasoning: Months are consumed by both table rendering and CSV export, so applying the rule where rows are built keeps display/export consistent without changing invoice records or schedule generation.

context: Horizontal scroll caused Unit Lokasi body cells to move while only No, Mitra ISP, and Unit Lokasi should be frozen.
options_considered: Change scroll container behavior
Increase z-index/background only
Align sticky body offsets and widths with colgroup/header
decision: Aligned body sticky offsets and widths with table colgroup/header: Mitra ISP width 180px, Unit Lokasi left offset 244px and width 280px.
reasoning: Browser measurement showed header Unit Lokasi stayed at left 277 while body Unit Lokasi moved from left 277 to 227 after scroll; body had left-[194px] despite preceding frozen columns totaling 244px. Correcting offsets fixes the actual movement.

context: Desktop sidebar collapse/expand was hidden behind clickable logo/header, making it hard for new users to discover.
options_considered: Only add tooltip to existing logo button
Add text helper inside expanded sidebar
Add explicit edge handle with chevron icon
decision: Added a visible round chevron handle on the sidebar edge with aria-label/title that changes between Ciutkan sidebar and Buka sidebar.
reasoning: An edge handle is the clearest familiar affordance for collapsible sidebars and remains visible in both expanded and collapsed states without taking menu space.

context: Animated sidebar collapse hint was considered too thin/subtle.
options_considered: Return to round button style
Add text label
Keep hint style but increase visual weight
decision: Increased hint footprint to 40x72px, strengthened gold pill background/border/glow, increased chevron size to 26px, and widened horizontal nudge distance.
reasoning: User wanted animated directional icon rather than a button; increasing contrast and size preserves that intent while improving discoverability.

context: User wanted sidebar collapse affordance as icon only, no background, positioned slightly outside sidebar edge.
options_considered: Keep subtle pill background
Move icon directly on sidebar border
Use icon-only affordance slightly outside edge
decision: Removed pill/background pseudo-element styling from sidebar collapse hint and positioned transparent chevron-only animated hit area at right:-28px.
reasoning: Matches requested visual: just the moving icon, no background, still clickable and accessible through existing button markup.

context: Fix Trash feature bugs around restore, permanent delete, role affordances, and missing contract_versions rows.
options_considered: Only fix UI display bugs
Add database RPC/transaction for all trash operations
Implement dependency-aware client-side Trash API while preserving current method calls
decision: Keep the existing api.trash method surface, but make Trash API whitelist tables, include contract_versions in list/stats, restore child rows for same deleted_at cascade groups, perform dependency-aware permanent deletes, and delete empty-trash rows sequentially child-before-parent. UI now scopes teknisi loading to route trash, hides permanent delete from teknisi, uses table-id keys, and displays null last-deleted timestamps as '-'.
reasoning: A database RPC would give stronger transactionality, but the current app already centralizes Trash operations in frontend/src/lib/api.js and the requested fix can be completed without schema work. Preserving api.trash list/restore/deletePermanently/emptyTrash avoids touching other modules while fixing the observed cascade and UI bugs.

context: Add delete controls to Activity Log.
options_considered: Only add per-row delete buttons
Add selected rows and delete-all controls in the existing list header
Create a separate bulk-management screen
decision: Added API methods activityLogs.deleteMany(ids) and activityLogs.deleteAll(), then implemented page-level checkbox selection, selected-count delete, and all-log delete controls in ActivityLogPage.
reasoning: The user asked specifically for delete selection and delete all. Keeping controls in the existing list header avoids a new route and matches the page's current operational layout. API methods are narrow and preserve existing list/create behavior.

context: Supabase performance improvement for many concurrent logged-in users, requested to proceed gradually without changing working features.
options_considered: Refactor frontend dashboard/notification queries immediately
Drop duplicate and unused indexes immediately
Start with transparent DB-only FK indexes and RLS initplan optimization
decision: Applied a low-risk database-only step: added missing FK indexes flagged by Supabase advisor and rewrote selected RLS policies to wrap auth.uid()/auth.jwt() in SELECT initplans. Left frontend query refactors, duplicate index drops, and policy consolidation for later stages.
reasoning: FK indexes and RLS initplan changes preserve application behavior while reducing per-row policy overhead and FK check cost. Frontend query restructuring and policy consolidation have larger behavioral blast radius and should be handled in separate validated steps.

context: Gradual Supabase performance optimization continuation after low-risk FK/RLS initplan step.
options_considered: Remove all unused indexes from advisor output
Remove only duplicate indexes with identical definitions
Skip index cleanup and proceed directly to frontend query refactor
decision: Applied step 2 to remove only two advisor-confirmed duplicate indexes, preserving the index names declared by the active maintenance scripts.
reasoning: Duplicate indexes add write and maintenance overhead without changing query behavior. Unused index removal is riskier because production traffic may not have exercised all paths yet.

context: Gradual Supabase performance optimization continuation focused on RLS policy overhead.
options_considered: Consolidate every table with multiple permissive policies at once
Consolidate only the four simple/key tables first
Leave RLS consolidation for later and refactor frontend queries
decision: Applied step 3 to consolidate SELECT RLS policies on notification_states, users, isp_user_accounts, and isps while preserving access semantics by splitting admin ALL policies into explicit non-SELECT policies and one combined SELECT policy per table.
reasoning: The four selected tables had simple policy predicates and were part of the earlier advisor warnings. Limiting scope reduces access-regression risk while removing duplicate SELECT policy evaluation on common auth/reference paths.

context: Gradual Supabase performance optimization continuation for core monitoring and billing tables.
options_considered: Consolidate every remaining table in one migration
Consolidate only core monitoring/billing tables first
Start frontend query refactor before finishing RLS cleanup
decision: Applied step 4 to consolidate SELECT RLS policies on customers, customer_isp_memberships, contracts, contract_versions, invoices, documents, customer_route_versions, and customer_route_points while preserving admin/teknisi/isp access semantics.
reasoning: These core tables are used by the heaviest customer, billing, dashboard, route, and notification query paths. Their policies follow a consistent admin/teknisi/isp pattern, so consolidation is low risk and reduces repeated SELECT policy evaluation without changing application behavior.

context: Complete safe Supabase performance advisor cleanup after user requested continuing until done.
options_considered: Drop every index reported as unused
Consolidate remaining RLS warnings and keep unused indexes for production observation
Start frontend query refactor immediately
decision: Applied step 5 to consolidate the remaining multiple permissive RLS SELECT policies on follow-up/history/entry-point tables. Stopped short of dropping unused indexes because the live database has almost no rows and several unused indexes are FK or future hot-path indexes, so the advisor signal is not sufficient for safe removal.
reasoning: Multiple permissive policies are clear structural overhead and can be fixed without changing access semantics. Unused-index removal would be speculative in a sparse database and could reintroduce FK warnings or degrade future query paths.

context: Frontend query optimization after completing safe Supabase advisor cleanup, focusing on high-frequency Supabase reads without changing existing workflows.
options_considered: Move notification/dashboard aggregation to SQL RPC immediately
Add broad persistent localStorage cache
Add short-lived in-memory cache with explicit invalidation for hot read paths
decision: Added short-lived in-memory read-through caches for api.isps.getAll and notificationsApi.list. ISP list cache has 60s TTL with invalidation after ISP, ISP entry point, and relevant trash mutations. Notification list cache has 15s TTL keyed by user/year/limit/includeResolved with invalidation after markRead/markResolved.
reasoning: Short-lived in-memory caching reduces duplicate Supabase requests under navigation/remount bursts while preserving data freshness and avoiding schema/function changes. It is lower risk than rewriting aggregation queries and does not alter UI data shape.

context: ISP login route sometimes showed 'ISP tidak ditemukan' or mismatched ISP detail after login.
options_considered: Keep relying on auth user_metadata.isp_id and sync metadata only
Infer current ISP from getAll() returning exactly one row
Add explicit current ISP account resolver backed by isp_user_accounts and clear cache on auth changes
decision: Resolve current ISP from public.isp_user_accounts via api.session.getCurrentIspAccount, use that mapping before user_metadata.isp_id, and clear session-scoped caches when auth user changes/login/logout.
reasoning: RLS uses isp_user_accounts as the source of truth while production auth metadata isp_id is null. Inferring from a cached list is fragile and can leak stale role/user context across logins. Resolving the mapping directly aligns frontend routing with RLS and clearing caches prevents previous-session ISP list reuse.

context: Monitoring page could show stale rows after failed loads, lose row detail navigation when App customer list was not loaded, accept invalid year filters, and render loading/empty rows with a fixed colSpan.
options_considered: Only fix the customer detail navigation bug
Add server-side abort controllers for Supabase requests
Use lightweight request sequence guards and year normalization in UI/API
decision: Added direct fallback navigation to customer detail by ID, guarded monitoring loads with request sequence refs, normalized monitoring years in UI and API, cleared stale billing/history/alert rows on current request failure, made table loading/empty colSpan role-aware, and selected contract versions by the filtered year for historical monitoring.
reasoning: The main failure modes are client-side state races and mismatched assumptions between monitoring's direct data source and App's global customer list. Sequence guards are simpler and sufficient for ignoring stale responses, while API-level year normalization protects other callers too.

context: High-severity bugs in ISP follow-up actions: workspace/detail count drift, central notifications masking local fallback, and ISP role unable to submit renewal responses because table writes are admin-only.
options_considered: Keep notifications reading isp_contract_rows renewal_file_url/response_file_url and only patch UI counts
Allow ISP role direct update policy on isp_renewal_follow_ups
Use a SECURITY DEFINER RPC for ISP response submission
decision: Use isp_renewal_follow_ups as the notification source for ISP renewal state, merge centralized and fallback ISP action items with dedupe in IspDetailPage, and route ISP response uploads through a limited submit_isp_renewal_response RPC while admin keeps direct update flow.
reasoning: The stale count came from split state between detail follow-ups and global notification rows. Direct table update for ISP would broaden write access beyond response fields, while a scoped RPC can validate ownership, renewal presence, decision values, and insert the next contract row when the response is lanjut.

context: Depwire security scan flagged TenantDetailPage.jsx as an architecture security risk because the large page mixed role/access logic with direct data access calls.
options_considered: Ignore the medium architecture finding because it was not an exploitable vulnerability
Split the entire 8k-line TenantDetailPage into multiple components and hooks
Apply a narrow boundary extraction for role policy and data access facade
decision: Extract tenant detail role policy into tenantDetailAccess.js and move direct lib/api usage behind tenantDetailData.js facade; TenantDetailPage now consumes access decisions and a tenant-detail data boundary instead of importing lib/api directly.
reasoning: A full page split is too broad for this security fix and risks regressions. The narrow extraction removes the exact auth/data-access mixing that Depwire flagged, keeps behavior stable, and gives future refactors a clear boundary for moving more handlers out of the page.
